area.description: Constant cross-sectional area assigned to each ideal well-mixed tank; no vessel drawing or measurement record is available.

height_max.description: Common tank overflow height used as a benchmark safety boundary; not verified against a physical vessel.

cv_out.description: Lumped gravity-outlet coefficient in q = cv_out * valve * sqrt(level); no valve-characterization data is available.

ua_loss.description: Lumped ambient heat-loss conductance applied independently to each tank; not identified from cooldown data.

heater_max.description: Maximum electrical power command per heater; no heater nameplate or calibration evidence is bundled.

pump_flow_max.description: Maximum normalized feed-pump flow; no pump curve or test record is bundled.

pump_power_max.description: Maximum electrical power used by the benchmark pump energy metric; not verified against metered consumption.

t_cold.description: Default feed-water temperature for the benchmark scenario.

t_amb.description: Default ambient temperature used by the lumped heat-loss model.

h_floor.description: Numerical volume floor used only to avoid division by zero in the temperature balance.

heater_min_level.description: Assumed minimum liquid level for the dry-heating interlock; requires device-specific validation before plant use.

temperature_trip.description: Assumed heater trip temperature for this liquid-water benchmark; not a certified protection setting.

temperature_hard_limit.description: Assumed simulation termination boundary, not a claim that an atmospheric water tank can physically operate at this temperature.

validity_domain.evidence_limit: No plant-identification dataset, equipment datasheet, or device validation record is bundled.
assumptions: single-phase incompressible liquid with water volumetric heat capacity
three identical constant-area, perfectly mixed tanks
instantaneous normalized actuators without sensor, valve, pump, or heater dynamics
gravity outlet flow proportional to the square root of nonnegative liquid level
